data.title: CBIZ targets $4.00-$4.10 adjusted EPS in 2026 while reaffirming $2.8B-$2.9B revenue outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CBIZ (CBZ) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “We entered 2026 with a clear plan, and our overall first quarter performance was in line with our expectations,” said (President, CEO & Director Jerry Grisko), adding, “Our organic growth improved throughout the quarter and is up sequentially compared to the fourth quarter.”
  • “With our solid start to the year, we are reaffirming our revenue, adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow targets while increasing our adjusted EPS outlook,” said (CEO Grisko), and he quantified integration-related headwinds: “We estimate that these temporary factors reduced reported organic revenue growth by approximately 200 basis points in the first quarter.”
  • “With Peter's appointment as Chief Information and Technology Officer and President of CBIZ Technology, we're making a deliberate convergence, one leader, one platform, one road map,” said (CEO Grisko).
  • “Just last week, we began the full rollout of our latest internal capabilities company-wide, moving from primarily AI-assisted workflows to more advanced agentic-based AI solutions,” said (Senior VP, Chief Strategy Officer & National Leader of Technology Peter Scavuzzo), and he added an example: “For year 1, our AI-based data extraction workflow is producing 20% efficiency with our anticipation in subsequent years that this efficiency will grow to 40%.”
  • “We are on track to achieve our target of increasing offshore hours from approximately 6% in 2025 to 10% in 2026,” said (CEO Grisko), adding, “Over the next several years… we plan to expand hours completed outside the U.S. to more than 20%.”
  • “Consolidated revenue increased 1.3% year-over-year to $849 million, with organic revenue growth of 1%,” said (Senior VP & CFO Brad Lakhia), and he added, “Adjusted diluted earnings per share was $2.50 compared to $2.33 in the first quarter of last year.”

Outlook

  • “We continue to expect revenue to be between $2.8 billion and $2.9 billion, representing 2% to 5% year-over-year growth,” said (CFO Lakhia).
  • “Adjusted EPS is now expected to be in the range of $4 to $4.10 per share, which assumes a weighted average fully diluted share count of approximately 60.5 million,” said (CFO Lakhia).
  • “Free cash flow guidance is unchanged and expected to be in the range of $270 million to $290 million,” said (CFO Lakhia).
  • Compared with the prior quarter’s outlook ranges, CBIZ increased adjusted EPS (from $3.75-$3.85 to $4.00-$4.10) and updated adjusted EBITDA to $465 million-$475 million (from $450 million-$460 million), while keeping revenue ($2.8 billion-$2.9 billion) and free cash flow ($270 million-$290 million) unchanged.

Financial Results

  • “Adjusted EBITDA increased $3 million year-over-year to $244 million, and adjusted EBITDA margin increased slightly by 10 basis points,” said (CFO Lakhia).
  • “Free cash flow improved $64 million year-over-year, primarily due to $53 million of proceeds received from the final purchase price adjustment,” said (CFO Lakhia), adding, “This improvement… enabled us to fund approximately $63 million in share repurchases through the end of April.”
  • “Net leverage decreased to approximately 3.4x compared to approximately 3.9x at the end of the first quarter of 2025,” said (CFO Lakhia).
  • “Financial Services… resulted in reported organic growth of 1.8%,” said (CFO Lakhia), adding, “Excluding this impact, first quarter organic growth would have been approximately 4%.”
  • “First quarter revenue was $108 million, representing a 4% decrease year-over-year,” said (CFO Lakhia), and he attributed the decline to project/contingent commission comparisons and “the unexpected departure of a single producer and his team in February.”

Q&A

  • Jeffrey Silber, BMO Capital Markets: asked whether clients could “unbundl[e] some of the services” and create “pricing pressure” via AI; Senior VP Scavuzzo responded, “I don't think the tools are able to provide the expertise and knowledge we can offer in the profession… So I don't see that as being a pressing concern.”
  • Jeffrey Silber, BMO Capital Markets: asked about AI margin levers; Senior VP Scavuzzo responded, “I think it's too early for us to speak on all of the things we're working on right now.”
  • Tom Wendler, Stephens: asked about the pace of producer additions after the Benefits and Insurance departure; CEO Grisko responded, “We are planning on having about 15% increase year-over-year.”
  • Tom Wendler, Stephens: asked about cross-servicing; CEO Grisko responded, “They have all of those arrows in the quiver now,” and CFO Lakhia added, “We are seeing a lot of really, really positive traction across the 2 segments.”
  • Tom Wendler, Stephens: asked about repurchase pace; CEO Grisko responded, “So we're going to remain active,” adding, “our valuation… is quite undervalued.”
  • Andrew Nicholas, William Blair: asked about pricing pushback; CEO Grisko responded, “We're highly confident in our mid-single-digit pricing… and are not hearing really pushback on that pricing.”
  • Andrew Nicholas, William Blair: asked whether improving conditions made management “more constructive”; CFO Lakhia responded, “a quarter doesn't make a year,” while also noting, “we'll be lapping some of the integration related… impacts in the first half.”
  • Faiza Alwy, Deutsche Bank: asked what drove organic improvement and about integration confidence; CEO Grisko responded, “we're not seeing the same conditions that we saw last year,” and CFO Lakhia added, “Our onboarding process for our clients has notably improved.”
  • Christopher Moore, CJS Securities: asked whether AI investment implies a middle-market competitive advantage and possible share gains; CEO Grisko responded, “I believe that there's a great opportunity to take market share,” and Senior VP Scavuzzo added, “AI and automation is strengthening our position and not weakening.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was neutral to slightly skeptical, with repeated questions on AI-driven disruption, pricing durability, and the Benefits and Insurance producer departure.
  • Management sentiment was slightly positive in prepared remarks and more measured in Q&A; CFO Lakhia tempered macro optimism with “a quarter doesn't make a year,” while CEO Grisko emphasized confidence in pricing and repurchases, saying, “So we're going to remain active.”
  • Versus last quarter’s tone, management sounded more execution-focused on near-term acceleration (AI rollout timing and integration productivity) rather than broad platform positioning, while analysts maintained similar lines of questioning around macro sensitivity and AI risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • CBIZ moved from describing integration as “largely behind us” (Q4) to highlighting operational proof points from the “first busy season as an integrated company” (Q1) and citing improved month-to-month productivity.
  • Guidance language shifted from establishing 2026 ranges (Q4) to reaffirming ranges and raising adjusted EPS in Q1; CFO Lakhia tied the EPS change to “a lower share count driven by our share repurchases through April.”
  • Analyst focus stayed consistent on pricing and macro-driven project work, but expanded in Q1 toward more specific AI questions (client unbundling, competitive price alternatives, and margin applications of agentic AI).

Risks and Concerns

  • “Results continue to reflect elevated but transitory client attrition related to the integration,” said (CFO Lakhia), and he added that it reduced Financial Services revenue by “approximately 200 basis points” in Q1.
  • “The unexpected departure of a single producer and his team in February” pressured Benefits and Insurance results, said (CFO Lakhia), adding, “We do not anticipate any similar departures,” and “we have successfully enforced” restrictive covenants.
  • “January started off a little bit more challenging… largely just because of the fact our teams were really working together for the first time in busy season,” said (CFO Lakhia), while stating, “We feel like we fully overcame that.”

Final Takeaway

CBIZ management characterized Q1 as “in line with our expectations,” reaffirmed 2026 revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and free cash flow ranges, and raised adjusted EPS guidance alongside continued share repurchases. Leadership framed the post-integration operating model as increasingly functional after the first integrated busy season, while emphasizing initiatives to lift margins and growth through agentic AI rollout and a stepped-up offshore delivery mix toward 10% of hours in 2026 and more than 20% over the next several years.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Initial jobless claims drop to below 200K in past week
data.description: Initial jobless claims for the week ended April 25 dropped to 189K from 215K in the previous week (revised from 214K) and trailed the 213K consensus, according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

data.title: Endeavour Mining plc Non-GAAP EPS of $1.53, revenue of $1.35B
data.description:
• Endeavour Mining plc press release (EDV:CA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.53.
  • Revenue of $1.35B (+29.8% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $880m.
  • Free cash flow of $613M.

data.title: NNN REIT FFO of $0.86 in-line, revenue of $240.42M beats by $2.03M
data.description:
• NNN REIT press release (NNN): Q1 FFO of $0.86 in-line.
  • Revenue of $240.42M (+4.1% Y/Y) beats by $2.03M.
  • Increased ABR by 6.9% over prior-year results to $934.6 million
  • Increased portfolio occupancy to 98.6%, an increase of 30 and 90 basis points over the prior quarter and prior year periods, respectively, with a portfolio weighted average remaining lease term of 10.1 years

  • Additional Highlights:

    • Increased AFFO per share guidance to a new range of $3.53 to $3.59
    • Increased Core FFO per share guidance to a new range of $3.48 to $3.54 vs $3.51 consensus

data.title: MPT in charts: Portfolio contraction continues in Q1, alongside a ~20% rise in rent and interest income
Cruise stocks buoyed as Royal Caribbean beats expectations, sets lofty goal for FY26
data.description:
Royal Caribbean (RCL) put in another strong quarter, enabling the cruise operator to beat Wall Street’s expectations, return ~1.1B to its shareholders, and drive shares higher into Thursday’s open.

And although the company continues to navigate geopolitical tensions influencing travel in the Mediterranean and West Coast of Mexico that will negatively impact the current quarter, Royal Caribbean (RCL) anticipates another 10% increase in revenue for FY26 and a double-digit improvement to its adjusted profit.

“We continue to execute on our innovation pipeline and broaden our vacation ecosystem in ways that further strengthen our long-term growth trajectory," Royal Caribbean CEO Jason Liberty said, noting the company’s recent launch of Royal Beach Club Santorini, the upcoming delivery of Legend of the Seas, recent orders for Icon VI and Icon VII ships, and the introduction of the Royal ONE credit card.

Excluding the elevated fuel costs, gross cruise costs per available passenger cruise day were down 1% during the quarter. Coupled with a 12.5% increase in revenue, the company’s profit improved 33% to $3.60 per share, both of which beat Wall Street’s expectations.

Additionally, gross margin yields increased 6.9% and net yields increased 3.6% year-over-year, mainly due to higher pricing across key products and onboard revenue.

To account for geopolitical events that continue to impact high-yielding itineraries for the Mediterranean and West Coast of Mexico in the second and third quarter, Royal Caribbean (RCL) expects Q2 adjusted EPS to be within a range of $3.88 and $3.93, down from a year ago and below the consensus estimate of $4.04.

For FY26, however, revenue is expected to increase by 10% year-over-year, net yields are seen up 2.3% to 3.3%, and adjusted EPS is forecasted to be up 10% to be within a range of $17.10 and $17.50 per share with a midpoint of $17.30 that exceeds the $17.15 estimate.    

The results and premarket gain in the stock are lifting peers, with Carnival Corp. (CCL), Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), and Viking Holdings (VIK) trading higher.

data.title: Halozyme appoints Darren Snellgrove as CFO
data.description:
• Halozyme Therapeutics (Nasdaq: HALO) said on Thursday it has appointed Darren Snellgrove as CFO, effective June 8, 2026.
  • Snellgrove joins from Johnson & Johnson, where he most recently led investor relations and previously served as CFO of its pharmaceuticals business.
  • He will oversee Halozyme’s financial operations and strategy, including capital allocation, corporate development and investor relations, reporting to CEO Helen Torley.
  • HALO +0.94% premarket to $63.65.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ECB holds interest rates steady
data.description:
The European Central Bank ((ECB)) held its interest rates steady for a sixth meeting, with the deposit rate kept at 2%, as widely expected.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Booz Allen invests in PDW to boost autonomous drone capabilities
data.description:
• Booz Allen Hamilton (NYSE: BAH) on Thursday said it has made a strategic investment in PDW to accelerate the development and production of autonomous drone systems.
  • PDW, based in Huntsville, Alabama, designs and manufactures unmanned aerial systems for defense and public safety and has the capacity to produce up to 100,000 drones annually.
  • The investment aims to combine Booz Allen’s expertise in AI and autonomy with PDW’s manufacturing capabilities to support scalable, domestically produced drone technologies for defense applications.
  • BAH +0.07% premarket to $76.50.
  • Source: Press Release
